questions.question: What is the primary function of a multiplexer (MUX) in a digital circuit?
options: To store a single bit of data.
To select one of several input signals and route it to a single output.
To combine multiple input signals into a single output using a logical AND operation.
To divide a clock signal's frequency.
To generate clock pulses.

questions.question: In a multi-processor system with shared memory, what is the primary challenge addressed by 'cache coherence protocols'?
options: Minimizing the latency of memory accesses by processors.
Ensuring that all processors have a consistent view of data in shared memory, even when cached locally.
Reducing the total power consumption of the memory subsystem.
Preventing illegal memory accesses and protecting operating system resources.
Increasing the overall bandwidth between the CPU and main memory.

questions.question: In the physical design flow of a synchronous digital IC, what is the primary objective of the Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS) stage?
options: To arrange standard cells into rows and connect them with power and ground rails.
To generate and distribute the clock signal throughout the chip with minimal skew and jitter.
To verify that all design rules (DRC) are met on the layout.
To extract parasitic resistances and capacitances from the layout.
To assign specific logic functions to available routing channels.

questions.question: High-Level Synthesis (HLS) is increasingly used in modern chip design flows. What is its primary function?
options: To perform detailed gate-level simulations to verify functional correctness.
To mathematically prove the equivalence between an RTL design and its specification.
To automatically generate a Register Transfer Level (RTL) description from an algorithmic description (e.g., C/C++).
To optimize the physical layout for power, performance, and area after placement.
To insert dedicated test structures for design-for-testability (DFT) purposes.

questions.question: In advanced semiconductor technologies, what reliability phenomenon refers to the transport of material caused by the gradual movement of the ions in a conductor due to momentum transfer from conducting electrons, leading to potential open circuits or short circuits over time?
options: Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI)
Hot Carrier Injection (HCI)
Electromigration (EM)
Time Dependent Dielectric Breakdown (TDDB)
Latch-up
